### Message

prd_review for wiring the real configured reviewer panel into mergeability calibration so S_full is measured separately from S_probe. All four promise contracts (P1 configured-panel wiring, P2 oracle isolation, P3 fail-closed availability, P4 replayable evidence) ground to real source seams. Genuine net-new work: configured_reviewers/evaluate_reviewer_panel have zero occurrences in mergeability_bench.py, so the production roster is not yet wired. Out-of-scope and report-only invariants explicit and verified hardcoded False. Accept.

### Confidence

- value: `0.84`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: All PRD promise contracts verified against real source seams via direct Read/Grep; net-new gap confirmed (zero occurrences of production reviewer seams in bench), ruling out FM-1.3 step-repetition; all 4 grill findings resolved with verified mappings (FM-2.4 satisfied). Held below 0.9 because prd_review is forward-looking with no execution receipt and one low-severity coherence tension (live-infra vs no-live-by-default) is deferred to downstream TDD/impl gates.

### Criteria

- All P1-P4 seams resolve to existing functions at named lines: PASS
- Production reviewer seams absent from bench (net-new, not step-repetition): PASS
- Report-only invariants verified hardcoded False in source: PASS
- Grill findings resolved with concrete, verified mappings: PASS
- Execution/validation receipt present: N/A at prd_review (forward-looking)

### Evidence

- ACCEPT prd_review: all 4 promise contracts (P1-P4) trace to verified real seams; anti-goals explicit; oracle isolation structural; report-only invariants pinned False in source

### Claims

- The full-gate path currently falls back to unavailable unless a panel is manually injected (reviewer_panel=None default at mergeability_bench.py:777)
- The configured production reviewer roster is not yet wired into the calibration bench (0 occurrences of configured_reviewers/evaluate_reviewer_panel in mergeability_bench.py)
- PRD promise contracts P1-P4 ground to real, existing seams
- Report-only invariants are already hardcoded False in the bench, so the PRD's non-applyability scope is enforceable

### Objections

- Low severity: PRD is forward-looking with no validation receipt (expected at prd_review). Mild tension between production wiring calling live reviewer infra (configured_reviewers) and Testing Decisions forbidding live Cursor/Codex by default; resolved via injectable adapter + explicit unavailable result + P3 fail-closed, deferred to TDD/impl.

- None recorded.

## event_id: 832421

- ts: `1782019037`
- kind: `dual_agent_interaction_message`
- gate: `issues_review`
- interaction_type: `gate_response`
- message_type: `gate_response`
- sender: `claude_code`
- recipient: `codex`

- persona_id: `claude_code.lead_worker`
- addresses: `event:832415`, `handoff:/Users/sam.zhang/Documents/codex-supervisor/.handoff/mergeability-real-panel-wiring-20260621.json`

### Message

Issues gate for wiring the configured reviewer panel into mergeability calibration. Three slices (ISS-1 panel adapter, ISS-2 oracle-isolation+report-only, ISS-3 replayable evidence) trace cleanly to all four PRD promises with no orphans in either direction. Each slice names a first public-boundary RED test that maps to a real tdd case; configured-panel mode is genuinely absent from supervisor source (current default returns reviewer_panel_not_configured at mergeability_bench.py:2159). All referenced seams verified real (run_paired_acceptance_pilot:770, supervisor_candidate_review arm:880-953, _public_input_oracle_refs:2745, reviewer_registry.py configured_reviewers:202/independent_reviewer_results_from_review_results:319/evaluate_reviewer_panel:339) and report-only guardrails hardcoded False in calibration report paths. FM-1.3 N/A: fresh HEAD eca0c3c7, distinct artifact, first issues_review for task. Accept.

### Confidence

- value: `0.84`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: Static trace confirms full bidirectional traceability, genuine RED (configured-panel absent, current fallback present), all seams real, and report-only guardrails real. Confidence capped below 0.9 because pytest cannot run pre-implementation and some ISS-2/ISS-3 ACs lean partly GREEN against pre-existing scaffolding, requiring TDD to pin the net-new boundary.

### Criteria

- issues->PRD traceability complete both directions: PASS
- first-RED tests map to real tdd cases and named symbols absent at HEAD: PASS
- referenced seams exist in source: PASS
- report-only guardrails real: PASS
- FM-1.3 step repetition check: N/A (distinct handoff/HEAD/artifact)
- executable verification of RED: NOT POSSIBLE at issues stage

### Evidence

- test_run_paired_acceptance_pilot_uses_configured_panel_when_requested (ISS-1, named, not yet written)
- test_configured_panel_unavailable_does_not_count_as_accept (ISS-1/P3)
- test_configured_panel_not_invoked_when_reviewer_packet_contains_oracle_material (ISS-2)
- test_configured_panel_report_records_reviewer_results_and_packet_refs (ISS-3)
- test_configured_panel_calibration_remains_report_only (ISS-2/P3)
- ACCEPT issues_review: traceability complete both directions, RED genuine, seams real, report-only guardrails real, FM-1.3 N/A

### Claims

- Issues slices trace to all four PRD promises with no orphan slices or orphan promises
- Configured-panel registry-invocation path is genuinely absent at HEAD; first-RED tests are genuinely red
- All referenced reviewer-registry and leak-detector seams exist in source
- Report-only / non-applyable guardrails exist and are hardcoded False, backing ISS-2 AC4
- FM-1.3 step repetition does not apply: distinct HEAD, distinct artifact sha, first issues_review for this task

### Objections

- Low severity: ISS-2 AC1 and ISS-3 AC1 partly GREEN-leaning because packet builder, leak invocation (2124), packet_sha256, and oracle_isolation_violations already exist; net-new is registry-invocation wiring
- Minor: no explicit dependency wave (ISS-1 implicitly precedes ISS-2/ISS-3)
- Minor: P3 double-attributed to ISS-1/ISS-2 and P4 to all three slices; no reverse coverage index in the issues doc

### Questions

- None recorded.

### Critical Review

`{"assumptions_to_verify": ["At TDD/implementation, the ISS-2 oracle-not-invoked test must assert the reviewer runner is not called, which requires the new invocation path to exist first (keeps it genuinely RED)", "ISS-3 evidence fields (reviewer result summaries, panel decision) are net-new beyond existing packet_sha256"], "contradictions_checked": ["Whether configured-panel mode already exists in source (it does not; docs-only)", "Whether reviewer registry seams named in PRD exist (all three exist in reviewer_registry.py)", "Whether report-only flags are actually hardcoded False (confirmed across four report contexts)", "Whether this handoff repeats source_run_id 8ebdbc89 (no: distinct HEAD/artifact, first issues_review)"], "decision": "accept", "evidence_refs": [], "missing_evidence": ["Executable RED run (cannot run pytest pre-implementation; tests not yet written)", "Explicit confirmation that ISS-3 reviewer-result-summary fields are absent today versus only packet_sha256 present"], "schema_version": "critical-review/v1", "severity": "low", "strongest_objection": "Pre-existing reviewer-panel scaffolding (packet builder ~2100-2128 with leak check at 2124, _normalise_reviewer_panel_result:2131, packet_sha256, oracle_isolation_violations) means several ISS-2/ISS-3 acceptance criteria describe behavior that partially exists, risking GREEN-leaning slices; the genuinely net-new work is wiring configured_reviewers/evaluate_reviewer_panel into the full-gate arm.", "what_would_change_my_mind": "Evidence that configured-panel registry invocation already exists in supervisor source (making ISS-1 RED vacuous), or that the named first-RED tests would pass against current code without new wiring."}`

### Message

TDD plan for wiring the configured reviewer panel into mergeability calibration is public-boundary-first at run_paired_acceptance_pilot:770 with genuine RED (configured_panel mode absent from supervisor/ and tests/), reuses real seams (leak detector, registry constructors, report-only flags), and covers every PRD promise and issue slice. ACCEPT with low-severity objections.

### Confidence

- value: `0.84`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: Boundary, seams, RED-genuineness, and full promise/slice coverage statically verified against source at HEAD eca0c3c7; only residual risk is partial GREEN-lean on t2/t3 and pytest not executed (approval-gated).

### Criteria

- Tests start at real public boundary run_paired_acceptance_pilot:770
- RED genuine: configured_panel mode 0 matches in supervisor/ and tests/
- All reused seams exist (leak detector, registry constructors, report-only flags)
- All 4 PRD promises and 3 ISS slices mapped with no orphans
- Oracle-isolation and forbidden report-only outcomes have dedicated tests

### Evidence

- test_run_paired_acceptance_pilot_uses_configured_panel_when_requested
- test_configured_panel_unavailable_does_not_count_as_accept
- test_configured_panel_not_invoked_when_reviewer_packet_contains_oracle_material
- test_configured_panel_report_records_reviewer_results_and_packet_refs
- test_configured_panel_calibration_remains_report_only
- accept

### Claims

- TDD plan is public-boundary-first per grill Finding 1
- configured-panel mode does not exist at HEAD eca0c3c7 -> RED genuine
- Fake reviewers injected below registry seam keep live reviewers out per grill Finding 2
- Report-only invariants remain hardcoded/computed False; t5 guards no-regression
- FM-1.3/FM-2.4/lead_invocation_failed do not apply to this first submission

### Objections

- t2/t3 RED partly weak: generic reviewer_panel path already enforces the asserted behaviors (unavailable->not-accept :2037, leak skips panel :2026-2032); only the configured-panel registry mode is genuinely new
- t2 RED is vaguely worded ('risks collapsing into unclear fallback') rather than a concrete failing assertion at the boundary
- t5 is GREEN-stays / no-regression guard: report-only invariants already non-applyable (:1283-1288)
- configured-panel mode API surface (new kwarg vs enum) is unspecified in the plan
- no reverse coverage index (test->promise given; promise->test only in impl plan)

### Questions

- None recorded.

### Critical Review

## event_id: 832432

- ts: `1782019212`
- kind: `dual_agent_gate_result`
- gate: `tdd_review`
- status: `accepted`
- attempts: `1`
- handoff_packet_path: `/Users/sam.zhang/Documents/codex-supervisor/.handoff/mergeability-real-panel-wiring-20260621.json`

### Summary

TDD plan for wiring the configured reviewer panel into mergeability calibration is public-boundary-first at run_paired_acceptance_pilot:770 with genuine RED (configured_panel mode absent from supervisor/ and tests/), reuses real seams (leak detector, registry constructors, report-only flags), and covers every PRD promise and issue slice. ACCEPT with low-severity objections.

### Decisions

- accept

### Objections

- t2/t3 RED partly weak: generic reviewer_panel path already enforces the asserted behaviors (unavailable->not-accept :2037, leak skips panel :2026-2032); only the configured-panel registry mode is genuinely new
- t2 RED is vaguely worded ('risks collapsing into unclear fallback') rather than a concrete failing assertion at the boundary
- t5 is GREEN-stays / no-regression guard: report-only invariants already non-applyable (:1283-1288)
- configured-panel mode API surface (new kwarg vs enum) is unspecified in the plan
- no reverse coverage index (test->promise given; promise->test only in impl plan)

### Specialists

- `lead-static-trace`: `accept` — objection: t2/t3 partly GREEN-lean: generic reviewer_panel path already enforces unavailable->not-accept (:2037) and leak->panel-not-invoked (:2026); only configured-panel registry mode is net-new

### Tests

- test_run_paired_acceptance_pilot_uses_configured_panel_when_requested
- test_configured_panel_unavailable_does_not_count_as_accept
- test_configured_panel_not_invoked_when_reviewer_packet_contains_oracle_material
- test_configured_panel_report_records_reviewer_results_and_packet_refs
- test_configured_panel_calibration_remains_report_only

### Claims

- TDD plan is public-boundary-first per grill Finding 1
- configured-panel mode does not exist at HEAD eca0c3c7 -> RED genuine
- Fake reviewers injected below registry seam keep live reviewers out per grill Finding 2
- Report-only invariants remain hardcoded/computed False; t5 guards no-regression
- FM-1.3/FM-2.4/lead_invocation_failed do not apply to this first submission

### Probes

- `P1`: `green` / `planning_artifact_boundaries_ok`
- `P2`: `green` / `worker_orchestration_invocation_ok`
- `P3`: `green` / `outcome_fidelity_ok`
- `P4`: `green` / `outcome_gate_decision_ok`
- `P_planning`: `green` / `planning_validation_ok`

## event_id: 832601

- ts: `1782019444`
- kind: `dual_agent_interaction_message`
- gate: `tdd_review`
- interaction_type: `review_response`
- message_type: `review_response`
- sender: `cursor`
- recipient: `codex`
- round_index: `1`
- persona_id: `cursor.independent_reviewer`
- addresses: `event:832436`

### Message

TDD plan is public-boundary-first at run_paired_acceptance_pilot with genuine RED for configured-panel registry wiring (mode absent at HEAD; default reviewer_panel_not_configured). t2/t3/t5 partially overlap existing generic panel path and tests, but t1/t4 pin net-new work and full PRD/issue traceability holds. Accept for implementation.

### Confidence

- value: `0.82`
- source: `cursor_self_reported`
- rationale: Static trace at HEAD eca0c3c7 confirms boundary, genuine t1/t4 RED, seam existence, and full traceability; confidence capped below Claude due to t2/t3/t5 GREEN overlap and vague t2 RED; no pytest execution (pre-implementation).

### Criteria

- Tests declared at run_paired_acceptance_pilot public boundary
- configured_panel mode absent from supervisor/ and tests/ at HEAD
- Default fallback returns reviewer_panel_not_configured
- Registry seams exist in reviewer_registry.py but unwired on paired-acceptance path
- All P1-P4 and ISS-1/2/3 mapped with dedicated tdd cases
- Report-only invariants verified hardcoded False in source

### Evidence

- test_run_paired_acceptance_pilot_uses_configured_panel_when_requested
- test_configured_panel_unavailable_does_not_count_as_accept
- test_configured_panel_not_invoked_when_reviewer_packet_contains_oracle_material
- test_configured_panel_report_records_reviewer_results_and_packet_refs
- test_configured_panel_calibration_remains_report_only
- accept

### Claims

- TDD plan begins at run_paired_acceptance_pilot per grill Finding 1
- configured-panel mode is genuinely absent from source -> t1 RED is real
- Generic full-gate path already has leak blocking and unavailable normalization; net-new work is registry-mode adapter
- t4 targets absent independent reviewer result summaries on configured-panel path
- t5 is no-regression guard against existing report-only tests
- Prerequisite gates prd_review and issues_review artifacts present and consistent

### Objections

- t2/t3/t5 RED partly weak: generic reviewer_panel path already enforces unavailable->not-accept (mergeability_bench.py:2034-2042) and leak->skip-panel (2026-2032); existing tests test_full_gate_unavailable_reviewer_does_not_count_as_accept and test_full_gate_calibration_report_cannot_create_applyable_policy_claim cover analogous behavior
- t2 RED vaguely worded ('risks collapsing into unclear fallback') vs t1's pinned reviewer_panel_not_configured assertion
- configured-panel mode API surface (kwarg vs enum) unspecified in tdd.md
- No promise->test reverse index in tdd.md (only in implementation-plan.md)
- P2 packet-absence partially covered by existing test_full_gate_reviewer_packet_excludes_oracle_material, not in declared acceptance_items

### Questions

- None recorded.

### Critical Review

## event_id: 832603

### Cursor Probe

- probe_id: `CURSOR`
- status: `green`
- reason: `cursor_review_ok`

### Cursor Outcome

TDD plan is public-boundary-first at run_paired_acceptance_pilot with genuine RED for configured-panel registry wiring (mode absent at HEAD; default reviewer_panel_not_configured). t2/t3/t5 partially overlap existing generic panel path and tests, but t1/t4 pin net-new work and full PRD/issue traceability holds. Accept for implementation.

Claims:

- TDD plan begins at run_paired_acceptance_pilot per grill Finding 1
- configured-panel mode is genuinely absent from source -> t1 RED is real
- Generic full-gate path already has leak blocking and unavailable normalization; net-new work is registry-mode adapter
- t4 targets absent independent reviewer result summaries on configured-panel path
- t5 is no-regression guard against existing report-only tests
- Prerequisite gates prd_review and issues_review artifacts present and consistent

Decisions:

- accept

Objections:

- t2/t3/t5 RED partly weak: generic reviewer_panel path already enforces unavailable->not-accept (mergeability_bench.py:2034-2042) and leak->skip-panel (2026-2032); existing tests test_full_gate_unavailable_reviewer_does_not_count_as_accept and test_full_gate_calibration_report_cannot_create_applyable_policy_claim cover analogous behavior
- t2 RED vaguely worded ('risks collapsing into unclear fallback') vs t1's pinned reviewer_panel_not_configured assertion
- configured-panel mode API surface (kwarg vs enum) unspecified in tdd.md
- No promise->test reverse index in tdd.md (only in implementation-plan.md)
- P2 packet-absence partially covered by existing test_full_gate_reviewer_packet_excludes_oracle_material, not in declared acceptance_items

Specialists:

- `Cursor Reviewer`: `accept`

### Message

Implementation plan to wire a configured reviewer-panel adapter into run_paired_acceptance_pilot so S_full is measured separately from S_probe, reusing reviewer_registry seams, blocking on oracle leaks, and preserving report-only invariants. RED genuine (configured_panel mode absent in src+tests), all seams verified real at HEAD eca0c3c7, traceability complete (5 plan tests==5 TDD tests, P1-P4 covered, no orphans). Accept.

### Confidence

- value: `0.84`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: All seams verified real at HEAD, RED genuine in both src and tests, traceability complete with no orphans, FM checks cleared. Capped at 0.84 because pytest was not run (approval-blocked), shasum verification was approval-blocked (content verified via Read instead), plan is thin, and t5 is GREEN-leaning.

### Criteria

- Files-to-touch exist: yes
- RED genuine (boundary/mode absent): yes
- Seams real and verified: yes
- PRD->TDD traceability complete, no orphans: yes
- FM-1.3/2.4 cleared: yes (distinct gate, no withheld objections)

- test_run_paired_acceptance_pilot_uses_configured_panel_when_requested
- test_configured_panel_unavailable_does_not_count_as_accept
- test_configured_panel_not_invoked_when_reviewer_packet_contains_oracle_material
- test_configured_panel_report_records_reviewer_results_and_packet_refs
- test_configured_panel_calibration_remains_report_only
- accept

### Claims

- Plan reuses existing reviewer_registry aggregation rather than duplicating it
- Oracle-leak block before reviewer invocation (Step 3) is net-new; existing code only annotates packet
- All files-to-touch exist; no orphan files introduced

### Objections

- Plan's Files-To-Touch lists prd.md and tdd.md which are mutable_by_worker=false in handoff; worker must treat as reference only, not edit
- Plan is thin: no per-step->test mapping, no dependency waves, no reverse traceability index
- t5 (report-only) is GREEN-leaning: invariants at 1283-1288 already hardcoded False, so the test guards existing behavior rather than new behavior

### Message

Independently review the implementation_plan gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Intent:
Wire the real configured reviewer panel into mergeability calibration so S_full is measured separately from S_probe without leaking oracle material or creating applyable policy claims.

### Confidence

- value: `None`
- source: `None`
- rationale: None recorded.

### Criteria

- None recorded.

### Evidence

- None recorded.

### Claims

- Plan reuses existing reviewer_registry aggregation rather than duplicating it
- Oracle-leak block before reviewer invocation (Step 3) is net-new; existing code only annotates packet
- All files-to-touch exist; no orphan files introduced
- decision:accept

### Objections

- Plan's Files-To-Touch lists prd.md and tdd.md which are mutable_by_worker=false in handoff; worker must treat as reference only, not edit
- Plan is thin: no per-step->test mapping, no dependency waves, no reverse traceability index
- t5 (report-only) is GREEN-leaning: invariants at 1283-1288 already hardcoded False, so the test guards existing behavior rather than new behavior

### Questions

- Do the planning artifacts, Claude outcome, and evidence receipts justify advancing this gate?

### Message

Implementation plan traces P1?P4 to five TDD tests with genuine RED on configured-panel mode (no `configured_panel` in src/tests; default `reviewer_panel_not_configured` at mergeability_bench.py:2159). Registry seams (`configured_reviewers`, `independent_reviewer_results_from_review_results`, `evaluate_reviewer_panel`) verified in reviewer_registry.py. Accept with low-severity objections: plan is thin, lists immutable prd/tdd as files-to-touch, and three of five tests largely duplicate existing full-gate tests or guard already-hardcoded report-only flags.

### Confidence

- value: `0.8`
- source: `cursor_self_reported`
- rationale: Core RED and seam verification confirmed independently; implementation-plan sha256 matches packet (dd1a2fbc). Capped below Claude because oracle blocking pre-exists at full_gate entry, three tests overlap existing coverage, plan omits API contract, and no pytest/runtime receipts exist at this planning gate.

### Criteria

- configured_panel mode absent from supervisor/ and tests/: confirmed
- Registry seams exist and are unreferenced in mergeability_bench.py: confirmed
- PRD?plan traceability complete (P1?P4, no orphan tests): confirmed
- Report-only invariants hardcoded False on run_paired_acceptance_pilot path: confirmed at 1038-1051
- implementation-plan.md sha256 matches planning_refs: confirmed

### Evidence

- test_run_paired_acceptance_pilot_uses_configured_panel_when_requested
- test_configured_panel_unavailable_does_not_count_as_accept
- test_configured_panel_not_invoked_when_reviewer_packet_contains_oracle_material
- test_configured_panel_report_records_reviewer_results_and_packet_refs
- test_configured_panel_calibration_remains_report_only
- accept

### Claims

- Only test_run_paired_acceptance_pilot_uses_configured_panel_when_requested and test_configured_panel_report_records_reviewer_results_and_packet_refs target clearly net-new behavior (mode wiring and registry-sourced reviewer summaries)
- Oracle leak blocking before panel invocation already implemented in _review_mergeability_candidate_full_gate, not solely at packet annotation
- Prior full-gate tests with injected reviewer_panel already cover unavailable-panel and report-only invariants for the same public boundary

### Objections

- Files-To-Touch lists prd.md and tdd.md marked mutable_by_worker=false in handoff; worker must treat as reference only
- Plan lacks per-step?test mapping, dependency waves, and explicit configured-panel API surface (param name/signature)
- t3 oracle-leak test is partially GREEN-leaning: _review_mergeability_candidate_full_gate already blocks panel_fn when leaks detected (mergeability_bench.py:2025-2032), not only annotates at packet build
- t4 unavailable and t5 report-only mirror existing test_full_gate_unavailable_reviewer_does_not_count_as_accept and test_full_gate_calibration_report_cannot_create_applyable_policy_claim; run_paired_acceptance_pilot already hardcodes metric_applyable/improvement_claim_allowed/policy_mutated/gate_advanced False at 1038-1051

### Questions

- None recorded.

### Critical Review

## event_id: 832839

- event_id: `832839`
- ts: `1782019868`
- kind: `tri_agent_cursor_review`
- gate: `implementation_plan`
- interaction_type: `cursor_review`
- gate: `implementation_plan`
- accepted: `True`
- model: `default`
- cursor_run_id: `run-7fbec276-ed84-4b3e-b264-e73686bc96c4`
- agent_id: `agent-d85919d8-d4aa-428e-b99a-5e2063a968f9`
- duration_ms: `41280`
- full_reasoning: `transcript.jsonl event 832839 transcript_tail`

### Cursor Probe

- probe_id: `CURSOR`
- status: `green`
- reason: `cursor_review_ok`

### Cursor Outcome

Implementation plan traces P1?P4 to five TDD tests with genuine RED on configured-panel mode (no `configured_panel` in src/tests; default `reviewer_panel_not_configured` at mergeability_bench.py:2159). Registry seams (`configured_reviewers`, `independent_reviewer_results_from_review_results`, `evaluate_reviewer_panel`) verified in reviewer_registry.py. Accept with low-severity objections: plan is thin, lists immutable prd/tdd as files-to-touch, and three of five tests largely duplicate existing full-gate tests or guard already-hardcoded report-only flags.

Claims:

- Only test_run_paired_acceptance_pilot_uses_configured_panel_when_requested and test_configured_panel_report_records_reviewer_results_and_packet_refs target clearly net-new behavior (mode wiring and registry-sourced reviewer summaries)
- Oracle leak blocking before panel invocation already implemented in _review_mergeability_candidate_full_gate, not solely at packet annotation
- Prior full-gate tests with injected reviewer_panel already cover unavailable-panel and report-only invariants for the same public boundary

Decisions:

- accept

Objections:

- Files-To-Touch lists prd.md and tdd.md marked mutable_by_worker=false in handoff; worker must treat as reference only
- Plan lacks per-step?test mapping, dependency waves, and explicit configured-panel API surface (param name/signature)
- t3 oracle-leak test is partially GREEN-leaning: _review_mergeability_candidate_full_gate already blocks panel_fn when leaks detected (mergeability_bench.py:2025-2032), not only annotates at packet build
- t4 unavailable and t5 report-only mirror existing test_full_gate_unavailable_reviewer_does_not_count_as_accept and test_full_gate_calibration_report_cannot_create_applyable_policy_claim; run_paired_acceptance_pilot already hardcodes metric_applyable/improvement_claim_allowed/policy_mutated/gate_advanced False at 1038-1051

Specialists:

- `Cursor Reviewer`: `accept` — objection: Plan thinness and test overlap (unavailable/report-only/oracle-block) reduce confidence that implementation will prove net-new behavior beyond configured-panel wiring and registry result summaries.

### Message

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Wire the real configured reviewer panel into mergeability calibration so S_full is measured separately from S_probe without leaking oracle material or creating applyable policy claims.

Implement this gate against the accepted source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Edit the worktree as needed; use decisions/objections to report whether execution is complete, blocked, or failed.

Runtime TDD test contract:
The supervisor runtime floor will verify that every TDD-named test below appears in supervisor-generated runtime evidence. Include tests/commands covering all of them in outcome.tests. Explicitly skipped tests must carry a recorded pytest skip reason; silently absent tests block the gate.
Use only canonical gate decisions (`accept`, `revise`, or `deny`). Do not return `accept_with_residual`; if test execution needs verification, declare the exact pytest commands/nodeids and let the supervisor runtime floor rerun them.
If the Claude Bash/test tool is unavailable but the implementation diff is complete, do not block solely on that local tooling outage. Return `accept` with test_status=`unknown`, list the exact pytest commands/nodeids, and make no tests-passed claim; the supervisor runtime floor is the authority and will block the gate on failing or missing tests.
- test_run_paired_acceptance_pilot_uses_configured_panel_when_requested
- test_configured_panel_unavailable_does_not_count_as_accept
- test_configured_panel_not_invoked_when_reviewer_packet_contains_oracle_material
- test_configured_panel_report_records_reviewer_results_and_packet_refs
- test_configured_panel_calibration_remains_report_only

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [deliverable_evidence_failed] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-provenance-trust-001): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [subprocess_failure] unknown (source_run_id=7b2b6001-e952-4cf4-8c47-f101e4eb8c72): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [timeout] worker_invocation (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
5. [timeout] worker_invocation (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.

- addresses: `event:832851`, `handoff:/Users/sam.zhang/Documents/codex-supervisor/.handoff/mergeability-real-panel-wiring-20260621.json`

### Message

Wired configured reviewer panel into run_paired_acceptance_pilot via ConfiguredReviewerPanelOptions and build_configured_reviewer_panel. S_full measured separately via real reviewer registry adapters with oracle-leak short-circuit, unavailable-not-accept normalization, replayable per-row evidence (reviewer_results, packet_refs, packet_sha256, panel_decision, s_probe_vs_s_full_disagreement), and report-only invariants preserved. 5 TDD tests pass; 8 existing reviewer-panel tests still pass.

### Confidence

- value: `0.9`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: Both the 5 TDD tests and the 8 regression tests pass against the implementation. Public-boundary tests exercise observable report rows and arm summaries, not internal helpers. Oracle-leak path verified by monkeypatched fake adapter that records calls. Slight residual: configured-panel mode delegates to existing registry helpers, so any defect in those helpers' aggregation could propagate undetected.

### Criteria

- 5 named TDD tests run and pass: met
- No regression in existing reviewer-panel/full-gate/paired-report tests: met
- Implementation diff covers PRD P1-P4 (panel wiring, leak isolation, fail-closed availability, replayable evidence): met
- Report-only invariants preserved end-to-end: met
- Public boundary unchanged for default callers: met

### Evidence

- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_run_paired_acceptance_pilot_uses_configured_panel_when_requested
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_configured_panel_unavailable_does_not_count_as_accept
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_configured_panel_not_invoked_when_reviewer_packet_contains_oracle_material
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_configured_panel_report_records_reviewer_results_and_packet_refs
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_configured_panel_calibration_remains_report_only
- supervisor/mergeability_bench.py
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py
- accept

### Claims

- 5 required TDD tests are present at the listed nodeids and pass under pytest 8.4.2 / Python 3.9.6
- Configured-panel mode is opt-in via reviewer_panel_mode='configured'; default 'custom' preserves backwards compatibility with prior reviewer_panel callable
- Oracle leak detection runs before configured reviewers are invoked
- S_full unavailable normalization keeps accepted_count at zero and surfaces reviewer_panel_unavailable gaming flag
- Report-only invariants remain hardcoded False for configured-panel arm; policy evolution derives no applyable proposal

### Objections

- None recorded.

### Questions

- None recorded.

`{"assumptions_to_verify": ["evaluate_reviewer_panel conservative aggregation behaves correctly under all-accept, mixed, and all-unavailable inputs (covered by fake-adapter tests, but real-adapter behavior assumed identical).", "_public_input_oracle_refs covers all leak vectors that could appear in a reviewer packet (existing leak detector reused, not modified)."], "contradictions_checked": ["Verified backwards compat: default reviewer_panel_mode='custom' still accepts the existing reviewer_panel callable and existing tests pass.", "Verified report-only invariants: configured_reviewer_panel_active does not flip metric_applyable to True (test_configured_panel_calibration_remains_report_only asserts policy evolution returns [])."], "decision": "accept", "evidence_refs": [], "missing_evidence": ["End-to-end run against a real ReviewerAdapter (e.g., invoke_cursor_agent) rather than fake adapters \u2014 out of scope for unit tests and explicitly noted as a risk in the implementation plan.", "Performance characterization of configured-panel pilot at non-trivial cohort sizes."], "schema_version": "critical-review/v1", "severity": "low", "strongest_objection": "The configured-panel adapter is primarily an orchestration layer over reviewer_registry helpers (configured_reviewers, independent_reviewer_results_from_review_results, evaluate_reviewer_panel). ### Message

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Wire the real configured reviewer panel into mergeability calibration so S_full is measured separately from S_probe without leaking oracle material or creating applyable policy claims.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Runtime TDD test contract:
The supervisor runtime floor will verify that every TDD-named test below appears in supervisor-generated runtime evidence. Include tests/commands covering all of them in outcome.tests. Explicitly skipped tests must carry a recorded pytest skip reason; silently absent tests block the gate.
Use only canonical gate decisions (`accept`, `revise`, or `deny`). Do not return `accept_with_residual`; if test execution needs verification, declare the exact pytest commands/nodeids and let the supervisor runtime floor rerun them.
If the Claude Bash/test tool is unavailable but the implementation diff is complete, do not block solely on that local tooling outage. Return `accept` with test_status=`unknown`, list the exact pytest commands/nodeids, and make no tests-passed claim; the supervisor runtime floor is the authority and will block the gate on failing or missing tests.
- test_run_paired_acceptance_pilot_uses_configured_panel_when_requested
- test_configured_panel_unavailable_does_not_count_as_accept
- test_configured_panel_not_invoked_when_reviewer_packet_contains_oracle_material
- test_configured_panel_report_records_reviewer_results_and_packet_refs
- test_configured_panel_calibration_remains_report_only

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [deliverable_evidence_failed] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-prod-confidence-001): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [missing_worker_signal] worker_invocation (source_run_id=cffe55b8-47b9-4cc0-b025-586aeb0ee5e9): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
4. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
5. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=f89bc699-1b4e-4713-95b7-d1493a3acfd8): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.

## event_id: 833210

- ts: `1782021341`
- kind: `dual_agent_interaction_message`
- gate: `outcome_review`
- interaction_type: `review_response`
- message_type: `review_response`
- sender: `cursor`
- recipient: `codex`
- round_index: `1`
- persona_id: `cursor.independent_reviewer`
- addresses: `event:833015`

### Message

Independent inspection confirms +623-line uncommitted diff wires configured reviewer panel for S_full separate from S_probe. All 5 acceptance nodeids pass locally (96s). Source verifies leak-before-invoke ordering, fail-closed unavailable semantics, packet-ref recording, and hardcoded report-only invariants. Accept with low-severity caveat: production configured_reviewers() path untested end-to-end.

### Confidence

- value: `0.88`
- source: `cursor_self_reported`
- rationale: Independently executed all 5 nodeids (5 passed). Static review confirms each acceptance invariant in source with non-vacuous tests. Deductions for uncommitted state and zero coverage of the production configured_reviewers() branch without test injection.

### Criteria

- 5 TDD tests present and substantive: yes
- Implementation backs each test invariant in source: yes
- Leak-before-invoke ordering verified at 2080-2087: yes
- Report-only invariant intact at 1093-1110 with empty policy proposals: yes
- Tests executed independently by Cursor reviewer: yes (5/5 passed)
- Work committed: no
- Production configured_reviewers() path tested: no

### Evidence

- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_run_paired_acceptance_pilot_uses_configured_panel_when_requested
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_configured_panel_unavailable_does_not_count_as_accept
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_configured_panel_not_invoked_when_reviewer_packet_contains_oracle_material
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_configured_panel_report_records_reviewer_results_and_packet_refs
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_configured_panel_calibration_remains_report_only
- supervisor/mergeability_bench.py
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py
- accept

### Claims

- Configured panel mode is net-new wiring through existing registry aggregation seams
- Oracle leak detection blocks panel invocation before any adapter runs
- Calibration report remains report-only with no applyable policy proposal path
- S_probe arm unchanged when configured panel enabled; S_full measured separately with disagreement tracking

### Objections

- Diff remains uncommitted at HEAD eca0c3c7; downstream merge requires a commit
- All five tests inject fake reviewers; default configured_reviewers() roster path has zero CI coverage
- Runtime test receipt claims pass but carries no captured pytest log in packet

### Questions

- None recorded.

- gate: `outcome_review`

- duration_ms: `173122`
- full_reasoning: `transcript.jsonl event 833212 transcript_tail`

### Cursor Probe

- probe_id: `CURSOR`
- status: `green`
- reason: `cursor_review_ok`

### Cursor Outcome

Independent inspection confirms +623-line uncommitted diff wires configured reviewer panel for S_full separate from S_probe. All 5 acceptance nodeids pass locally (96s). Source verifies leak-before-invoke ordering, fail-closed unavailable semantics, packet-ref recording, and hardcoded report-only invariants. Accept with low-severity caveat: production configured_reviewers() path untested end-to-end.

Claims:

- Configured panel mode is net-new wiring through existing registry aggregation seams
- Oracle leak detection blocks panel invocation before any adapter runs
- Calibration report remains report-only with no applyable policy proposal path
- S_probe arm unchanged when configured panel enabled; S_full measured separately with disagreement tracking

Decisions:

- accept

Objections:

- Diff remains uncommitted at HEAD eca0c3c7; downstream merge requires a commit
- All five tests inject fake reviewers; default configured_reviewers() roster path has zero CI coverage
- Runtime test receipt claims pass but carries no captured pytest log in packet

Specialists:

- `Cursor Reviewer`: `accept`

### Transcript Tail

Independently executed all 5 nodeids (5 passed). Static review confirms each acceptance invariant in source with non-vacuous tests.
